data.title: White House Clears Rule That Could Open $10 Trillion 401(k) Market to Bitcoin and Crypto
data.description: Key TakeawaysWhite House review cleared a Labor Department rule that could allow crypto in $10T 401(k) retirement market.The proposal would amend fiduciary guidance under ERISA to include Bitcoin and alternative assets.Rule classified as “economically significant,” signaling major potential market impact.Move follows Trump executive order pushing broader access to digital assets in retirement plans.The White House has cleared a key regulatory review of a proposed rule that could open the $10 trillion U.S. 401(k) market to Bitcoin and other digital assets, marking a significant step toward mainstream crypto adoption in retirement portfolios.The Office of Information and Regulatory Affairs (OIRA), part of the Executive Office of Donald Trump, completed its review on March 24, allowing the Department of Labor to proceed with formally publishing the proposal.If finalized, the rule would amend fiduciary guidance for retirement plans governed by the Employee Retirement Income Security Act (ERISA), potentially permitting plan sponsors to include cryptocurrency and private equity as investment options within participant-directed 401(k) plans.The proposal follows a prior executive order from Trump directing federal agencies, including the Securities and Exchange Commission and Treasury Department, to explore expanding access to alternative assets—such as crypto—within retirement accounts.OIRA classified the rule as “economically significant,” indicating it could have a material impact on the U.S. economy, typically defined as exceeding $200 million annually.The development comes as retirement savings continue to grow. Data from Fidelity Investments shows the average 401(k) balance reached $144,400 in Q3 2025, up 9% year-over-year, while IRA balances rose to $137,902.If implemented, the rule could significantly expand institutional demand for Bitcoin by enabling retirement funds to allocate capital into crypto markets, potentially reshaping long-term inflows and market structure.

data.title: Bank of England Deputy Governor: UK Bond Market Stable, No Evidence of Forced Selling
data.description: The Deputy Governor of the Bank of England, Briden, has stated that the UK government bond market has been operating normally, with no signs of forced selling. According to Jin10, Briden emphasized the stability of the market, dismissing concerns about any abnormal activities or pressures that might lead to forced sales. The statement comes amid ongoing scrutiny of global financial markets, where volatility and economic uncertainties have raised questions about the resilience of various sectors. Briden's remarks aim to reassure investors and stakeholders about the health of the UK's bond market, highlighting its ability to function smoothly despite external challenges.

data.title: Traders Consider Impact of Trump's April Tariff Reversal
data.description: Traders are currently evaluating the implications of U.S. President Donald Trump's decision in April to reverse tariffs, as they consider whether to sell. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing deliberations among market participants. The tariff reversal has introduced uncertainty into the market, prompting traders to reassess their strategies in light of potential economic shifts. This decision by the Trump administration is seen as a significant factor influencing trading activities and market dynamics.

data.title: Crypto Stocks Fall Pre-Market as Coinbase, MicroStrategy, Circle Decline
data.description: Key TakeawaysCrypto-related stocks trade lower pre-market, led by Circle (CRCL) down 2.3%.MicroStrategy (MSTR) falls 1.66%, while Coinbase (COIN) drops 1.88%.Bitcoin miners and related equities also decline, reflecting weaker risk sentiment.Broader move signals cautious positioning ahead of market open.Crypto-related stocks in the U.S. are trading broadly lower in pre-market hours, tracking cautious sentiment across digital asset markets.Circle (CRCL) declined 2.3%, leading losses among crypto-linked equities. Other notable movers include ALTS (-1.48%), UPXI (-3.36%), BMNR (-2.45%), and SBET (-2.34%).Major crypto proxies also edged lower. Shares of MicroStrategy fell 1.66%, while Coinbase dropped 1.88%. Bitcoin mining stocks also weakened, with MARA down 2.17%.The declines come amid subdued momentum in Bitcoin and broader risk assets, as macro uncertainty and geopolitical developments continue to weigh on sentiment.Data cited from msx.com, a decentralized real-world asset (RWA) trading platform offering tokenized exposure to U.S. equities and ETFs, indicates the pullback is widespread across crypto-linked names.The pre-market weakness suggests investors remain cautious ahead of the U.S. trading session, with crypto equities continuing to act as a high-beta proxy for Bitcoin price movements and overall market risk appetite.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Criticizes NATO's Inaction on Iran
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed criticism towards NATO countries for their lack of action regarding Iran, which he described as a 'crazy country' that has been militarily destroyed. According to Jin10, Trump emphasized that the United States does not require any assistance from NATO but urged to 'never forget' this significant moment.

data.title: Startale Group completes $63 million Series A funding round, with SBI and Sony participating.
data.description: Startale Group has completed a $63 million Series A funding round, with SBI Group investing $50 million and Sony Innovation Fund investing $13 million. This round of funding will be used to advance its on-chain infrastructure development, including Layer 2 networks, stablecoins, and tokenized securities. Startale stated that the funds will also be used to expand into Asian on-chain capital markets and promote the deployment of related products.

data.title: Bank of England Deputy Governor: Benchmark Interest Rate Remains Tight as Labor Slack Increases
data.description: Bank of England Deputy Governor, Ben Broadbent, has stated that the current benchmark interest rate is maintaining a tight stance amid increasing labor market slack. According to Jin10, Broadbent emphasized the importance of monitoring economic indicators closely to assess the impact of monetary policy on the labor market. He noted that while the interest rate is designed to curb inflation, the growing slack in the labor force could pose challenges to achieving economic stability. Broadbent's comments come as the Bank of England continues to navigate complex economic conditions, balancing inflation control with the need to support employment. The Deputy Governor's remarks highlight the ongoing debate within the central bank regarding the appropriate level of interest rates to foster sustainable economic growth.

data.title: Germany Considers Further Measures to Address Rising Fuel Costs
data.description: German Economy Minister Katherina Reiche announced that the coalition government is contemplating additional strategies to mitigate the effects of escalating fuel prices. Bloomberg posted on X that this consideration follows the approval of an initial package by lawmakers aimed at addressing the issue. The government is actively exploring options to provide relief to consumers and businesses affected by the rising costs. The initial measures are part of a broader effort to stabilize the economy amid fluctuating energy prices.

data.title: Markets Show Concern as Conflict in Iran Continues
data.description: As the conflict in Iran extends into its second month, financial markets are beginning to exhibit signs of concern. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential implications for global economic stability. Financial regulators are advised to closely monitor the situation, as prolonged instability could have significant repercussions on international markets.

data.title: Iran's Oil Revenue Surges Amid Ongoing Conflict
data.description: Iran has reportedly gained significant additional income from oil sales since the onset of the war. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the conflict has led to increased demand and higher prices for oil, benefiting Iran's economy. This development comes as global markets continue to react to geopolitical tensions, influencing energy prices worldwide. The situation underscores the complex interplay between international conflicts and economic outcomes, particularly in the energy sector.

data.title: Brent Crude Surges Above $100 Amid Strait of Hormuz Closure
data.description: The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz has driven Brent crude prices above $100 per barrel throughout much of March, marking an increase of over 32% since February 28. According to NS3.AI, this situation has led to fuel emergencies in several countries. In Australia, more than 500 gas stations have completely depleted their fuel supplies. The Philippines has declared a national energy emergency, while South Korea has implemented a five-day vehicle rotation for the public sector, affecting approximately 1.5 million vehicles. Meanwhile, India has secured around 60 million barrels of Russian crude for delivery in April, as global fuel supply chains continue to face disruptions.

data.title: Iran Reviews U.S. 15-Point Plan, Pakistan's Foreign Minister Says
data.description: Iran is currently reviewing a 15-point plan proposed by the United States, according to Pakistan's Foreign Minister. The details of the plan have not been disclosed, but it is part of ongoing diplomatic efforts involving multiple countries. According to Jin10, the plan is under consideration by Iranian authorities, reflecting the complex geopolitical dynamics in the region. The outcome of this review could have significant implications for international relations and regional stability. Further developments are awaited as discussions continue.

data.title: QCP: Bitcoin remains range-bound, with the market still driven by macroeconomic and geopolitical factors.
data.description: QCP released a market report stating that Bitcoin is currently hovering around $71,000, exhibiting a consolidation trend rather than significant downward pressure. The report points out that the Middle East situation has brought a geopolitical risk premium, but Bitcoin has performed relatively steadily. Net outflows of on-chain funds indicate that funds are more inclined to withdraw from exchanges rather than sell. In the options market, implied volatility has decreased, the curve maintains a slight premium, and downside hedging demand remains but there has been no panic. QCP believes that before macroeconomic and geopolitical factors become clearer, Bitcoin will continue to maintain a news-driven range-bound trading pattern.

data.title: Germany Plans EU Strategy to Challenge U.S. Tech and Pharmaceutical Firms
data.description: Germany is reportedly preparing a strategy for the European Union to exert pressure on U.S. technology companies, pharmaceutical suppliers, and manufacturers amid ongoing tensions with U.S. President Donald Trump. Bloomberg posted on X, citing sources familiar with the matter, that the plan aims to address disputes with the U.S. administration by targeting key sectors. The initiative reflects Germany's growing concerns over trade imbalances and regulatory challenges posed by American firms. The move comes as the EU seeks to strengthen its position in negotiations and protect its economic interests. Further details on the strategy are yet to be disclosed, but it underscores the EU's intent to assert its influence in global trade discussions.

data.title: OECD Planned to Raise Global Economic Growth Forecast Before Iran Attack
data.description: The Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) had intended to revise its global economic growth forecast upwards prior to the recent attack in Iran. According to Jin10, the OECD's decision was influenced by improving economic indicators worldwide. However, the unexpected geopolitical tensions arising from the incident in Iran have introduced new uncertainties, prompting a reassessment of the economic outlook. The OECD is now closely monitoring the situation to evaluate its potential impact on global markets and economic stability.

data.title: Chow Tai Fook Expands into Australian Market with Sydney Store Opening
data.description: Chow Tai Fook Jewellery has announced its entry into the Australian market with the opening of its first store at Westfield Sydney. According to Ming Pao, the Vice Chairman of Chow Tai Fook Jewellery Group, Kent Wong, stated that the launch of the Sydney store marks a significant milestone in the brand's transformation journey, bringing its rich heritage and craftsmanship to the international stage. Chow Tai Fook aims to establish a strong connection with the Australian community by offering jewelry that combines artistry, innovation, and timeless elegance.

data.title: ECB's Commitment to Price Stability Highlighted by Guindos
data.description:
The European Central Bank (ECB) remains steadfast in its mission to maintain price stability, according to Vice President Luis de Guindos. Bloomberg posted on X that Guindos emphasized the ECB's dedication to controlling inflation, which is crucial for economic stability in the eurozone. He noted that the ECB is prepared to take necessary measures to ensure that inflation remains within target levels.

Guindos highlighted the importance of monetary policy in achieving these goals, stating that the ECB will continue to monitor economic indicators closely. The central bank's approach is aimed at fostering a stable economic environment that supports growth and employment.

The ECB's commitment comes amid ongoing economic challenges, including fluctuating energy prices and geopolitical tensions that could impact inflation rates. Guindos reassured that the ECB is equipped to address these issues through strategic policy adjustments.

The focus on price stability is part of the ECB's broader strategy to support the eurozone's economic recovery and maintain confidence in the financial system. Guindos's remarks underscore the central bank's proactive stance in navigating complex economic landscapes.

data.title: AI Tools Increase Efficiency of Crypto Attacks, Security Experts Warn
data.description: AI tools are increasingly being used to make cryptocurrency attacks more efficient, according to a December benchmark. According to NS3.AI, AI agents were able to exploit 63% of 405 previously compromised smart contracts. Security experts have highlighted that outdated and poorly maintained DeFi code is particularly vulnerable to these attacks. Meanwhile, defenders are only beginning to adopt similar AI tools to enhance security measures.

data.title: France to Invite India, South Korea, Brazil, and Kenya to G7 Summit
data.description: The French Presidency has announced that the upcoming G7 Leaders' Summit, scheduled to be held in June in Évian, France, will extend invitations to India, South Korea, Brazil, and Kenya. According to Jin10, this move aims to foster broader international cooperation and dialogue on global issues. The inclusion of these countries reflects an effort to engage with emerging economies and address shared challenges on the global stage.

data.title: Germany's Energy Minister Warns of Potential Fuel Shortage if War Persists
data.description: Germany's Energy Minister has expressed concerns over the country's fuel supply, stating that if the ongoing conflict continues, Germany may face a shortage in its fuel plans. According to Jin10, the minister highlighted the potential impact of prolonged warfare on energy resources, emphasizing the need for strategic planning to mitigate risks. The situation underscores the broader challenges faced by nations in securing energy supplies amid geopolitical tensions.
